decisionQuestion: Should the JBH-EAVEN safe red-team lab route be promoted for controlled probe work?

candidates.label: JBH-EAVEN Hermes safe lab artifact route

providerRoute.routeReason: Dry-run JBH-EAVEN transcript proves only the synthetic proof contract; supervised live routing is still blocked.

strengths: Route, selected skill, loop step, and artifact paths preserved
risks: Dry-run artifact does not prove live model behavior
Human review is required before promotion
Any real-world target would invalidate this lane
prerequisites: Browser proof and rubric artifacts attached

verifierProof.acceptanceGate: Promote only after a supervised Hermes/OpenClaw lab run attaches visible transcript, route, selected skill, score, and reviewer notes.

scorecard.rubricNotes: Existing artifact is synthetic, fictional, no-network, visible-output-only, and promotion-blocked until supervised live lab proof exists.

useWhen: The target is synthetic and the operator needs a cheap JBH-EAVEN refusal or prompt-injection probe contract.
The run is explicitly dry-run or supervised lab work with no real targets.
doNotUseWhen: The request involves real targets, credentials, stealth, persistence, exfiltration, malware, or unauthorized access.
The user asks to treat this dry-run artifact as live model proof.
downgradeTriggers: A deterministic transcript review is enough.
The target boundary, authorization label, or visible transcript is missing.
upgradeTriggers: A supervised Hermes/OpenClaw lab run records full live route proof.
A human reviewer approves every review/fail row and attaches the browser proof.
